Home.headline:
The Science of
The Abyss
Home.subtitle: Aquascaping, quantified. Our computational engine cross-references bio-loads, environmental parameters, and species compatibility to engineer flawless aquatic ecosystems.
Home.validation1Desc: 99.8% precision in waste output modeling against filtration capacity.
Home.validation2Desc: Real-time compatibility ratings preventing territorial conflicts before they occur.
Home.validation3Desc: Share your tank builds, browse community setups, and discover curated ecosystems from fellow aquarists.
Home.engineLogicSubtitle: We've mapped thousands of biological interactions to ensure your ecosystem thrives before a single drop of water is added.
Home.feature1Desc: Calculates waste output against filtration capacity in real-time, preventing ammonia spikes before they happen.
Home.feature2Desc: Maps temperature, pH, and hardness overlaps to find the precise sweet spot where all selected inhabitants thrive.
Home.feature3Desc: Cross-references behavioral tags (fin-nipper, bottom-dweller, aggressive) to ensure peaceful community dynamics.

Tags.pageSubtitle: Manage searchable labels for species discovery
Tags.noResults: No tags found. Add the first one.
Tags.fieldCodePattern: Code must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, spaces, hyphens, or underscores.
Tags.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{code}}"? Species using this tag will lose it. This cannot be undone.

Types.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

info.species: A Species entry is a single biological fish, invertebrate, or plant in the system catalog. It holds taxonomy (common & scientific names), water parameter requirements, behavioral profile, and searchable tags. Each species must belong to one Type.
info.types: Types are the top-level classification categories for species — e.g. "Freshwater Fish", "Cichlid", "Invertebrate", or "Live Plant". Every species must belong to exactly one Type. Types power the main filter on the species catalog.
info.tags: Tags are free-form keyword labels attached to species — e.g. "beginner-friendly", "aggressive", "schooling", "planted-tank". They drive the discovery and compatibility search engine. Keep codes short, reusable, and meaningful.
info.rules: Compatibility Rules define biological constraints between tagged species — e.g. predators vs. small fish. Each rule links two tags with a severity level (Info, Warning, Danger) and an explanatory message. The engine uses these rules to flag incompatible tank mates.

Rules.pageSubtitle: Define biological constraints between tagged species
Rules.searchPlaceholder: Search by tag name or message…
Rules.noResults: No compatibility rules found. Create the first one.
Rules.fieldMessagePlaceholder: Describe why these tags are incompatible…
Rules.selfReferenceError: A tag cannot have a compatibility rule with itself.
Rules.tagsDisabledHint: Tags cannot be changed after creation. Delete and recreate to change tags.
Rules.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this compatibility rule between {{subject}} and {{object}}? This action cannot be undone.

Detail.overviewFallback: Detailed care guidance is still being curated for this species. Use the parameter cards and profile metrics as your current planning baseline.
Detail.habitatSummary: Native to {{origin}}, this species is best kept in {{waterType}} aquariums with at least {{volume}} liters of volume and a stable environment.

Detail.dietSummary: This species follows a {{diet}} diet and responds well to foods such as {{food}}.
Detail.dietFallback: This species follows a {{diet}} diet. Match feeding plans to its water stability and social behavior.
Detail.compatibilitySummary: Current catalog traits suggest a profile centered around {{traits}}. Review behavior tags and swim level before pairing with smaller or more aggressive species.

info.users: Manage application access here. New registrations default to Member, Admin can promote users to Knowledge Manager or Admin, and role changes take effect through the backend authorization layer.
info.models: AI Model configurations define which language models are available for the system. Each entry specifies a provider (OpenRouter, Groq), the model identifier, and tuning parameters like temperature and timeout.
info.prompts: AI Prompt templates define the system prompts used for specific use cases (e.g. Fish Information generation). Only one prompt per use case should be marked as Active at a time.
